Good Afternoon Community Partner,

My name is Joe Allen, Founder of Central Valley Prospects , the fastest-growing high school girls' basketball platform in the country, built to give historically overlooked Central Valley athletes the same recognition and opportunities as players in major markets like Los Angeles, Sacramento, and the Bay Area. In just 18 months, we’ve helped generate over 60 full-ride scholarship offers, have over 400 college coaches following our Instagram, built 35 strategic partnerships, held two wildly successful camps, held a commitment party, launched internship opportunities across multiple professions, and created real momentum that’s changing lives at zero cost to the selected athletes.

Now we’re bringing that same energy and impact to Las Vegas as we proudly announce the Inaugural CVP Vegas Elite Camp, hosted by UNLV Women’s Basketball star Meadow Rowland, a proud Fresno native and the reigning Newcomer of the Year and 6th Woman of the Year. This isn’t just another camp, as we’re making it a true first-of-its-kind experience in Las Vegas. College players from multiple universities will serve as hands-on camp counselors, and elite coaches from across the region will be involved. Every session will deliver high-level instruction in an environment built on inspiration, access, and mentorship. We’re creating exposure, confidence, and unforgettable memories for young athletes, all at a price families can genuinely afford.

On June 12th and 13th, we’ll host girls in grades 1st through 8th for just $99 total per athlete. Every camper receives a customized camp jersey, two-hour daily training sessions grouped by grade, elite instruction, daily food and drinks throughout camp, signed posters, and, on Day 2, two live, officiated games that give players a real game-day experience. This is a premium-level camp delivered at an intentionally affordable cost.

To make this experience possible at this price point, we’re seeking aligned companies that believe in empowering young women, supporting community growth, and attaching their brand to excellence and opportunity. I've included the sponsorship opportunities below and attached the photos to help you understand the visual impact. All contributions are fully tax-deductible through the CV Prospects Foundation. Our sponsors receive significant brand exposure across our Instagrams. Central Section Outlet generates 20 million monthly views, and Central Valley Prospects generates 1.2 million monthly views, putting your brand in front of a powerful, highly engaged regional audience. We will have media coverage from all local television stations at the camp.

Title Sponsor $3,500
As the face of the event, your brand receives premier logo placement across all marketing materials, social media campaigns, digital promotions, media coverage, an opportunity to give away the MVP awards, a signed jersey, and on-site branding. Your company will be the driving force behind the camp, with maximum visibility and community impact.

Presenting Sponsor $2,500
You’ll receive elevated branding across promotional materials and a strong on-site presence, prominent logo placement in digital campaigns, social media features, and recognition in major announcements.

Jersey Sponsor $1,500
Your company name will be fully branded on one of the official customized camp jerseys worn by athletes, creating lasting visibility in photos, videos, social media content, and throughout the community long after the camp concludes.

Jersey Patch Sponsor $750
Your company name will be a patch sponsor on one of the official customized camp jerseys worn by athletes, creating lasting visibility in photos, videos, social media content, and throughout the community long after the camp concludes.

Retractable Banner Sponsor $500
Your logo will be displayed on a banner as attendees arrive and will also be featured in all event-related content.

T-Shirt Sponsor $350
Your logo will be featured on the back of shirts worn by all camp counselors, including current college athletes, providing consistent exposure throughout the event and across shared media content.

To see the impact we’ve made and the brand exposure we deliver with our camps, please take a moment to explore our work:

Subject: Service & Billing Resolution – Stay 2/21–2/25
Dear GM,
I am writing regarding both the service issue and billing reconciliation from my stay February 21–25.
As previously reported during our stay, the Junior Suite upgrade did not meet expectations due to the smaller window next to the bed having only blinds and no blackout curtains. Because we were attending a conference and required daytime sleep, this significantly impacted our ability to rest and work effectively.
Engineering confirmed no drapes were available, and a supervisor offered a resolution of:

 *
Resort fee credit of $62.30 per night for two nights (total $124.60)
 *
A $50 food credit

We were also awaiting communication regarding a potential room change; however, no follow-up occurred, and we ultimately remained in the room without further resolution.
Separately, the billing was not handled properly. The original total was $797.50. After the agreed credits, the corrected total should be:
$797.50
– $124.60 (resort fees)
– $50.00 (food credit)
= $622.90
Instead, my card was charged $890.32, and the adjustments were not processed until I personally reached out today. This required unnecessary follow-up on my part and should have been reconciled at checkout.
To resolve this fully, I am requesting:

 1.
A revised final folio reflecting all agreed credits,
 2.
Confirmation that the full $124.60 resort fee credit has posted,
 3.
Confirmation that the $50 food credit has been applied,
 4.
Written confirmation of the final settled balance.

The room issue affected the quality of our stay, and the billing delay compounded that frustration. I trust this can now be properly corrected.
I appreciate your prompt attention and written confirmation once completed.
Thank you,

LaSalle Strong

• Smoking & Cannabis Policy

In compliance with Nevada law and federal regulations, the possession, use or consumption of cannabis in any form is strictly prohibited anywhere on our premises, including guest rooms, public areas, and casino floors. • Oversized Vehicle Parking
Due to limited space, guests are not permitted to park any oversized vehicle overnight, including but not limited to buses, RVs, U-hauls, and trailers.
• Scooters/Motorized PUVs
Scooters and motorized personal utility vehicles (PUVs) are not allowed on property for recreational use. We do not provide rentals. If an accommodation needs to be made for a non-recreational PUV, please see a hotel manager for a special permit.
• Pets
With the exception of service dogs, pets are not permitted on property. Service dogs must remain with their owner at all times. Dogs left unattended in a room will be reported to Security, and the guest will be asked to check out.

Due to limited space, guests are not permitted to park any oversized vehicle overnight, including but not limited to buses, RVs, U-hauls, and trailers.
• Scooters/Motorized PUVs
Scooters and motorized personal utility vehicles (PUVs) are not allowed on property for recreational use. We do not provide rentals. If an accommodation needs to be made for a non-recreational PUV, please see a hotel manager for a special permit.
• Pets
With the exception of service dogs, pets are not permitted on property. Service dogs must remain with their owner at all times. Dogs left unattended in a room will be reported to Security, and the guest will be asked to check out.

inbound_body:
CAUTION: External email, think before you click!

Hello Sahara LV Team!

My name is Isabel; I am a booking agent here at Urban Talent. I was reaching out to introduce myself and our agency to you! We are a full-service talent agency located in the Mountain West. We have offices in Utah, Las Vegas, and Denver. We help clients source talent for projects such as promotions, tradeshows, social media campaigns, advertisements, and more!

I would love to discuss what opportunities you have coming up and where Urban would make sense to support those goals!

Cheers!

Cheers!

Tina, Kendall and Isabel
Urban Booking Team I 801-539-0800
https://www.urbantalent.com/

[1712166996692]

[cid:image002.png@01DCAD76.2DC56690]

Booking Terms and Conditions:*
RATES: Rates are negotiated by the booking agent on a project Basis. An agency fee charge of fifteen percent (15%) will be added to all Talent fees and usage fees.
OVERTIME: Bookings are based on half and full days. Overtime is any additional hour or any part thereof, that extends beyond the half or full day time period and is billed at 1.5 times the hourly rate in one hour increments.
NON-UNION ELECTRONIC MEDIA: On set still photos for print require an additional and separate usage agreement and rate.
USAGE: All rights granted are contingent upon receipt of full payment. An additional fee will be assessed for extended use, exclusivity, use of actual names and or other specialized uses.
RELEASES: Client will not ask Talent to sign any release without the Agency's permission. Releases must be approved by the booking agent with at least 24 hours™ notice. Any release signed without Agency permission or consent will be considered void. Releases are valid upon receipt of full payment.
Talent may not be contacted by Client at any time unless approved by the booking agent

*These conditions apply to all bookings unless negotiated prior to booking with the Booking Agent

inbound_subject: [EXTERNAL] - Attn: PR TEAM - Collaboration Opportunity – DJ Soul Fusion Las Vegas Stay (March 19–22)
approved_response_pattern: Answer the guest's policy or informational question directly, use current property facts only, and invite follow-up if the guest needs more detail.
inbound_summary: Influencer/manager requesting a media or partner rate for a DJ's stay in exchange for social media content featuring the property (reels, stories, tags).
reason: Influencer collaboration request requiring PR/marketing team assessment of audience metrics, content value, and approval of comp or discounted rates.
inbound_body:
CAUTION: External email, think before you click!

Hi Sahara Team,

My name is Stacey Rae and I manage Australian DJ and performer DJ Soul Fusion (Laree Gadenne).

Laree will be travelling to Las Vegas this March to perform during her U.S. tour, and we are currently finalising accommodation for her stay while she is in the city.

She will be in Las Vegas from March 19th to March 22nd, and we would love to explore the possibility of collaborating with SAHARA Las Vegas during this visit.

We particularly love the boutique feel and unique style of the rooms at SAHARA, and believe they would feature beautifully as part of the visual storytelling we will be creating during the trip.

As part of Laree’s visit, we will be documenting her Las Vegas experience across social media, sharing content with her audience in the lead-up to the performance and throughout the stay. This will include reels, stories and posts highlighting the venues, experiences and places she stays while in Vegas.

We would love to feature SAHARA Las Vegas as part of this trip and were wondering if your team may be open to providing a media or partner rate for Laree’s stay during these dates.

In return, we would be very happy to include your property as part of the Vegas trip content through:

• Instagram reels and story content filmed at the hotel, showcasing Laree enjoying the property including the pool, boutique rooms and key amenities, or any specific areas you would like featured.
• Tagging and promoting SAHARA Las Vegas across social media platforms
• Featuring the hotel as part of the “DJ Soul Fusion in Las Vegas” trip coverage and lead-up campaign

Laree regularly performs at large public and festival events in Australia, including crowds exceeding 10,000 people, and has headlined major events such as Queensland New Year’s Eve celebrations, major Christmas Carols events, and the Miss Australia Pageant Finals.

We would love the opportunity to showcase SAHARA Las Vegas as part of this exciting milestone trip performing in Las Vegas.

If this is something your team may be open to discussing, please feel free to reach out and I would be happy to provide further information.

Thank you very much for your time and consideration.

Warm regards,

Stacey Rae
Manager – DJ Soul Fusion
www.djsoulfusion.com
sample_response: Thank you for reaching out to SAHARA Las Vegas. Your media collaboration inquiry has been forwarded to our Public Relations team for review. If there is interest, a team member will contact you directly. We appreciate you thinking of us.

Dear Sir or Madam,

 Hope you’re doing well. I'm a big fan of your hotel and experiences. Do you have time to speak this week? I’m reaching out to inquire about the best way to connect with your entertainment team. I’ve included an overview of my initiatives for sharing and internal review. I look forward to hearing from you.

Make An Impact Series:
This is a global lifestyle brand I founded, powered by a team of music artists. Our latest collaborations this month were with Ralph Lauren, the Los Angeles Rams, and SoFi Stadium. The next edition will take place in Santa Clara and San Francisco during Super Bowl weekend, anchored by a marquee live music event at Westfield Valley Fair with citywide activations, signature moments, and community engagement that drives sustained visibility and momentum. I’m working closely with the Cities of Santa Clara and San Francisco, their convention and visitors bureaus, mayoral offices, local universities, sports and cultural organizations, and community boards to bring the Series to life.

Why this matters for you:
Engage in a dynamic ecosystem where experiences, content, and communities amplify each other. Partners gain visibility across multiple touchpoints while co-creating initiatives that build cultural relevance, deepen engagement, and create lasting value.

Make An Impact Club:
This is our global superfan team made up of students, influencers, and celebrities across schools and communities. Members get early access to original music, merchandise, private events, and exclusive collaborations. We are currently collaborating with MrBeast, who has already licensed original music from our music artist team in his YouTube content. Beyond music licensing, we’re co-creating new internships and job opportunities for our superfans tied directly to his YouTube channel — including roles like video editors, thumbnail designers, and graphic designers. These opportunities offer hands-on experience in the creator economy while building career pathways alongside fan engagement. This collaboration also engages his audience of over one billion followers, amplifying our reach across youth-driven digital channels.

Why this matters for you:
The Club is a grassroots extension of the Series, turning audiences into active brand ambassadors and cultural leaders who sustain momentum year-round. You’ll gain access to a highly engaged network of tastemakers across campuses, digital platforms, and creator spaces. Programming can be co-created to support product launches, drive brand awareness, or cultivate ambassador initiatives — giving you authentic ways to build your brand through community-rooted influence.

Consulting and advisory board services:
With over a decade of experience in entertainment, partnerships, and brand development, I specialize in driving growth through strategic execution. I’m currently executive producing a $42 million feature film, Winning Streak, leading fundraising and creative strategy for a project at the intersection of music, sports, and storytelling. Once the raise is complete, I will also appear in the film and help develop the official soundtrack. To amplify its reach, we’ll activate my superfan communities at colleges and universities in filming locations. The project already features A-list talent, major sports leagues, and athlete partners, with compelling opportunities for brand integration, equity participation, and long-term expansion.

Why this matters for you:
Whether seeking short-term guidance or a long-term partnership, I bring a 360° approach to strategy, storytelling, and results. There are meaningful ways to get involved — creatively, as a brand collaborator, or strategic partner — particularly with my current feature film project. The goal is to transform attention into action and turn brand visibility into lasting equity.

Best,
Eddy

My name is Hugo Lapalme, and I am writing regarding my most recent stay at your hotel. This is not my first time staying with you. I visited many times in 2016 and even got married at your property on August 9th, 2016. Ever since, whenever I visit Las Vegas, I have always chosen your hotel.

When I finally received my room, it immediately smelled strongly of cigarette smoke. I called the front desk, was transferred to security, but no one came. I called again, and was told housekeeping would be sent to address the smell, but no one arrived. The odor remained throughout the evening.

By 9:00 p.m., the smell was still unbearable, and I had no choice but to check out early. In total, I spent less than three hours in the room.

Given my loyalty to your hotel and the very poor experience this time, I sincerely hope you will remove the room charges for this stay. I also want to emphasize that I plan to return in November and would like to continue staying at your property, provided this issue is addressed.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your reply and to a better experience on my next visit.

Sincerely,

CAUTION: External email, think before you click!

Dear sir/madam,

I stayed at the Sahara Hotel from Tuesday 24th February until Tuesday 3rd March. My wife Lisa and I stayed in room 1233 and my son William stayed in room 1234. I booked through British Airways which covered the cost of the rooms and this included a 50% reduction in the resort fees - meaning the resort fees were $27 + tax = $30.61 per day. I stopped for 7 days meaning a total cost of $214.27 per room.

I have checked my credit card bill and I can see that I have been charged three amounts of $214.27 for resort fees. However, the fees are per room, so I should have only been charged two amounts of $214.27 (one for each room). I have attached photos of my credit card showing you have taken three payments:
2300 Sahara Hotel £165.93
2300 Sahara Hotel £165.93
2300 Sahara Hotel £165.77
When I view each of these transactions they all show the same dollar value as $214.27 (see the second attached image).

This is the third time I have emailed about this. Please can you refund $214.27 to my credit card. If you do not, I will be forced to raise a dispute with my credit card company to recover the money from you.

Kind regards

Ian Glover
